summary: The actual screenshot depicts a gameplay map with a centered, large combat event modal displaying summary details, which aligns more closely with the screen description than the wireframe. However, significant mismatches exist in modal size, content, buttons, and additional elements, indicating the actual UI has evolved beyond the wireframe baseline. The wireframe is partially suitable as a style reference but inadequate for detailed layout validation.

correctElements.requirement: Global wireframe style requirements: Dark starfield/game-map background
correctElements.evidence: Both actual and wireframe show a dark background with star systems and connections.

correctElements.requirement: FR-UI-SCREEN-COMBAT-EVENT-DIALOG: Gameplay map with centered combat event modal
correctElements.evidence: Top bar present in both with turn, phase, and active player information.
Resource indicators (POP, MET, FUEL) visible in both, positioned similarly at top-left.

correctElements.requirement: Global wireframe style requirements: semi-transparent black panels, thin green borders
correctElements.evidence: Map key panel on right side with green border in both.
Modal dialog is centered in both actual and wireframe.

incorrectElements.requirement: FR-UI-SCREEN-COMBAT-EVENT-DIALOG: Larger dialog with combat summary, region/body context
incorrectElements.expected: Small modal with bounded scroll content
incorrectElements.actual: Large modal spanning much of the screen with detailed combat summary and graphics
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Actual modal is significantly larger and includes integrated graphics and text not present in wireframe, deviating from wireframe baseline.

incorrectElements.requirement: Global wireframe style requirements: terminal-style labels
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Title text differs, which may indicate a change in UI flow or event triggering.

incorrectElements.actual: Specific combat details including attackers, defenders, and region
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Wireframe uses generic placeholder, while actual has dynamic content; this mismatch limits validation of content structure.
Button labels and actions differ, suggesting a different interaction model not captured in wireframe.

incorrectElements.requirement: Global wireframe style requirements: fixed screen-relative geometry
incorrectElements.actual: Systems including Sirius and Canopus, no Arcturus; additional graphics
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Missing system in actual and extra elements like pointers, affecting geometric comparison.
Actual includes dynamic increments not in wireframe, potentially indicating unvalidated data binding.

incorrectElements.requirement: Global wireframe style requirements: simple rectangles for controls
incorrectElements.actual: Debug text at top (e.g., Planets F1 Dbg:Off ...)
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Extra debug elements in actual not present in wireframe, which could be a development artifact but affects cleanliness.

incorrectElements.expected: Selected panel on right with content
incorrectElements.actual: No selected panel; map key instead
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Wireframe includes a selected panel not in actual, indicating missing or changed UI component.

incorrectElements.requirement: Global wireframe style requirements: terminal-style labels
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Phase name differs, which might be contextual but highlights wireframe not representing current game state.

incorrectElements.requirement: TR-UI-AUDIT-AGENTS-001: Consider AGENTS-README-FIRST for process requirements
incorrectElements.actual: Actual includes dynamic elements like debug text and increments
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Residual risk: Wireframe lacks dynamic state representation, limiting validation of runtime behaviors as per repository process in AGENTS-README-FIRST.

wireframeSuitability.why: The wireframe provides a basic style and layout baseline at the correct resolution, satisfying FR-UI-AUDIT-001, but it uses placeholders and incorrect buttons/size, making it inadequate for validating the detailed combat summary and actions in FR-UI-SCREEN-COMBAT-EVENT-DIALOG. It does not fully represent the screen description, reducing its effectiveness for precise comparison.
missingRequirements: Detailed representation of combat summary and region/body context
Correct button actions (OK/Start instead of BACK/NEXT)
Dynamic elements like resource increments and debug visibility
recommendedWireframeChanges: Increase modal size to match 'larger dialog' description
Update content to include example combat summary and graphics
Change buttons to VIEW/Ok/Cancel or equivalent per requirements
Remove placeholders and add representative dynamic content
Ensure map systems and panels match expected game state
